verdicts.issue: Author given as 'Lowe, D.G.' (initials only). Per §4a, full first name should be used when canonically known. Should be 'Lowe, David G.'

publisher = {MLSys} is not a real publisher. MLPerf Training Benchmark appeared in Proceedings of MLSys 2020. Per §3 publisher should be a real organization (e.g., 'mlsys.org' or omit). Also `x-verified-source` URL contains LaTeX-escaped underscore (`paper\_files`) which is wrong for a URL string in a bib field.

Author list ends with 'others' rather than the full attested authorship. Per §4a, full author lists are expected. Also booktitle should be expanded per §2 venue convention to include '(NeurIPS Datasets and Benchmarks Track)'. Title should also be Title Cased: 'Multilingual Spoken Words Corpus'.

suggested_fix.title: Multilingual Spoken Words Corpus
suggested_fix.author: Mazumder, Mark and Chitlangia, Sharad and Banbury, Colby and Kang, Yiping and Ciro, Juan Manuel and Achorn, Keith and Galvez, Daniel and Sabini, Mark and Mattson, Peter and Kanter, David and Coleman, Greg and Jeffries, Bruce and Lai, Liangzhen and Yi, Andrew and Reddi, Vijay Janapa
suggested_fix.booktitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 35 (NeurIPS 2021) Datasets and Benchmarks Track

verdicts.issue: Key year says 2024 but entry year=2023 and the paper appeared at NeurIPS 2023 Datasets and Benchmarks Track. Author list truncated to 'and others' (full author list is 33+, including William Gaviria Rojas, etc.). Recommend renaming key to mazumder2023dataperf. .qmd propagation: every [@mazumder2024dataperf] reference must be updated.

suggested_fix.note: expand author list with Karla\v{s}, Bojan and Gaviria Rojas, William and Diamos, Sudnya and Diamos, Greg and He, Lynn and Parrish, Alicia and Kirk, Hannah Rose and Quaye, Jessica and Rastogi, Charvi et al.

verdicts.issue: Key claims 1956 (the date of the workshop) but entry year=1955 (the date the proposal was authored). Both are defensible: the proposal is dated 31 August 1955 but the project ran in summer 1956. The AI Magazine reprint cites it as 1955 in the byline. Mismatch is intentional but may render as (McCarthy et al., 1955) in citations even though the historical attribution is 1956 (Dartmouth Conference).

suggested_fix.note: consider year=1956 to match key, with a note that the proposal was drafted in 1955

verdicts.issue: Author = 'Netflix' for a website citation; Metaflow is currently maintained by Outerbounds and the Netflix-originated open-source project. Acceptable; minor - corporate author should be in protected braces: {{Netflix}} per §1 (proper noun preservation).

Author = 'Mirhoseini, Azalia and others' truncates the canonical authorship of an ICML 2017 paper. Per §4a, full author list expected. Also key uses underscores (non-standard convention; most other entries use camelCase or surnameYearWord). entry type @article but venue is ICML conference; should be @inproceedings. Missing publisher (PMLR) and pages.

suggested_fix.author: Mirhoseini, Azalia and Pham, Hieu and Le, Quoc V. and Steiner, Benoit and Larsen, Rasmus and Zhou, Yuefeng and Kumar, Naveen and Norouzi, Mohammad and Bengio, Samy and Dean, Jeff
suggested_fix.booktitle: Proceedings of the 34th International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML)

verdicts.issue: Duplicate of mitchell2019modelcards (same paper: Mitchell et al. 'Model Cards for Model Reporting', FAT* 2019, DOI 10.1145/3287560.3287596). One should be removed and citations consolidated. .qmd propagation: every [@mitchell2019model] OR [@mitchell2019modelcards] reference must be updated to the surviving key.

suggested_fix.note: consolidate with mitchell2019modelcards (recommend keeping mitchell2019modelcards as the more descriptive key); rename requires updating every [@mitchell2019model] reference in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).

verdicts.issue: Duplicate of mitchell2019model (same DOI 10.1145/3287560.3287596). Booktitle uses literal LaTeX `\textasteriskcentered` and embeds 'FAT* '19:' prefix; cleaner form would just be 'Proceedings of the Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT)'.

suggested_fix.note: consolidate with mitchell2019model; if kept, simplify booktitle to 'Proceedings of the Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT)'

verdicts.issue: Key suffix says 2021 but entry year=2020 and arXiv:2002.11054 was submitted Feb 2020. Also published as CGO 2021 paper, but body uses arXiv field/preprint pattern. Either rename key to mlir_framework_2020 or update entry to the CGO 2021 publication. .qmd propagation: every [@mlir_framework_2021] reference must be updated.

suggested_fix.note: Either match arXiv (2020) or upgrade body to the CGO 2021 conference paper: Lattner et al., 'MLIR: Scaling Compiler Infrastructure for Domain Specific Computation', CGO 2021.

verdicts.issue: URL https://mlcommons.org/ is the generic landing page, not the MLPerf Power benchmark page. Per §4 'Every URL listed must resolve to the cited paper (no 404s, no redirects to a generic landing page).'

Key claims 1965 (the original Electronics article) but entry's date=1998 and DOI 10.1109/jproc.1998.658762 corresponds to the IEEE Proceedings 1998 reprint of Moore's 1965 paper. Volume/issue/pages (86/1/82-85) match the 1998 reprint. The bibliography would render this as (Moore, 1998) under most styles even though the key suggests 1965. Author given as 'Moore, G.E.' (initials), should be 'Moore, Gordon E.' per §4a.

suggested_fix.author: Moore, Gordon E.
suggested_fix.note: either change year to 1998 to match content (with title 'Cramming More Components Onto Integrated Circuits (1965)'), or replace entry with the original 1965 Electronics article

verdicts.issue: Missing year field (only `date = {2021-02-02}`); per §4 metadata, year is the canonical lookup field. Author 'Moore, Gordon' is incomplete (Gordon E. Moore).

Key 'moreau2018relay' suggests Moreau is first author, but the entry's author list starts with Roesch, Jared. The actual paper (MAPL '18 'Relay: A New IR for Machine Learning Frameworks') was authored by Roesch, Lyubomirsky, et al. — Moreau is not a listed author at all. Silent corruption per §5 (key/content drift). .qmd propagation: every [@moreau2018relay] reference must be updated.

suggested_fix.note: First author is Jared Roesch; key surname token should match. Update all [@moreau2018relay] citations.

verdicts.issue: Author given as 'Foundation, Apache Software' — incorrect surname/given inversion. Should be a corporate author with protected braces: '{Apache Software Foundation}'.

Key 'neural_architecture_search_paper' is a generic placeholder unlike the conventional surnameYear pattern (per §5). Should be renamed to elsken2019nas (JMLR publication is 2019, vol 20). .qmd propagation: every [@neural_architecture_search_paper] reference must be updated. Year missing from entry.

Entry has volume=34, pages=19075--19090 which corresponds to NeurIPS 2021 Datasets and Benchmarks Track publication, but journal field reads 'arXiv preprint arXiv:2103.14749' and entry type is @article. Inconsistent: should be @inproceedings with NeurIPS booktitle, OR drop volume/pages and keep arXiv.

suggested_fix.booktitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34 (NeurIPS 2021) Track on Datasets and Benchmarks

verdicts.issue: Title 'NVIDIA CUDA [Slides]' with author 'Lippuner, Jonas' refers to a 2019 LANL slide deck (DOI 10.2172/1532687). The key 'nvidia_cuda_toolkit' suggests it should be the official NVIDIA CUDA Toolkit documentation, not third-party slides. Possible key/content drift but cannot confirm intent without seeing the citing prose.

suggested_fix.note: Consider replacing with official NVIDIA CUDA Toolkit Documentation or rename key to lippuner2019cuda

verdicts.issue: Key uses mixed case 'tensorRT' inconsistent with project key conventions (lower-snake elsewhere, e.g., nvidia_triton). Author 'NVIDIA' should be in protected braces ({NVIDIA}). Also a near-duplicate of nvidia2024tensorrt (same product, different vintage) — consider consolidation.
Near-duplicate of nvidia2024triton — both cite NVIDIA Triton Inference Server. Consider consolidating to a single canonical entry.

Author = 'Corporation, NVIDIA' — surname/given inversion. Should be corporate author with protected braces: '{NVIDIA Corporation}'. Same issue affects nvidia2020ampere, NVIDIA2020nvlink, nvidia2021cudnn.

Key year says 2020 but entry date=2021-03-01 (IEEE Micro vol 41, issue 2). The Choquette et al. 'NVIDIA A100 Tensor Core GPU: Performance and Innovation' paper appeared in IEEE Micro Mar/Apr 2021. Per §5, key year should match within 1 year — borderline (1-year gap is allowed but the Crossref date is 2021).

suggested_fix.note: Or update year=2021. May conflict with nvidia_cusparse_block / NVIDIA2020 — disambiguate.

verdicts.issue: Author = 'Corporation, NVIDIA' (inversion). Also a near-duplicate of NVIDIA2020 (same A100 architecture whitepaper, same URL). Consolidate.

Author = 'NVIDIA, Corporation' (inverted). URL https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/nvlink/ is a marketing landing page, not a technical report. Per §4, 'Every URL listed must resolve to the cited paper (no 404s, no redirects to a generic landing page).'

suggested_fix.author: {NVIDIA Corporation}
suggested_fix.note: Replace with a stable technical document URL or whitepaper PDF.

verdicts.issue: Author = 'Corporation, NVIDIA' inversion. Also URL https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/cudnn/developer-guide/index.html now redirects (NVIDIA refactored docs structure in 2024); verify URL liveness.

Key year says 2022 but entry date=2023-05 and DOI 10.1109/mm.2023.3256796 is IEEE Micro vol 43 issue 3 (May/June 2023). Choquette 'NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU: Scaling Performance' is a 2023 paper, not 2022. Per §5, this is a >1 year mismatch — error level.

suggested_fix.note: Update all [@nvidia2022h100] references in source files.

verdicts.issue: Missing url field (only embedded in howpublished as `<...>`); the howpublished field uses angle brackets which render literally. Missing x-verified-source. Per §4, URL should be in url field.

Entry type @article with journal=CoRR, but the paper was presented at the NIPS 2017 ML Systems Workshop. Either keep as arXiv preprint (journal/booktitle inconsistent — both fields populated), or convert to @inproceedings with booktitle 'Workshop on ML Systems at NIPS 2017'. Schema mixing (article+booktitle field).

suggested_fix.booktitle: Workshop on ML Systems at NIPS 2017

verdicts.issue: Author = 'Intel, Corporation' (surname/given inversion). Should be '{Intel Corporation}' per §1 corporate author convention.
Author = 'Microsoft' should be in protected braces ({Microsoft}) per §1 proper noun protection.

SILENT CORRUPTION per §5: key 'openimages_website' implies the Open Images dataset (Krasin/Kuznetsova et al.), but entry body is 'BS-80K: The first large open-access dataset of bone scan images' by Huang et al. — a completely unrelated bone-scan medical imaging dataset. Citations as [@openimages_website] would render as '(Huang et al., 2022)' about bone scans, not Open Images. This is exactly the silent-corruption class flagged in bib-check.md §5.

suggested_fix.title: The Open Images Dataset V4: Unified image classification, object detection, and visual relationship detection at scale
suggested_fix.author: Kuznetsova, Alina and Rom, Hassan and Alldrin, Neil and Uijlings, Jasper and Krasin, Ivan and Pont-Tuset, Jordi and Kamali, Shahab and Popov, Stefan and Malloci, Matteo and Kolesnikov, Alexander and Duerig, Tom and Ferrari, Vittorio
suggested_fix.note: CRITICAL: replace entire entry. Update all [@openimages_website] citations in source files (.qmd, .tex, etc).

verdicts.issue: Author list ends with 'others' rather than the canonical full author list. Per §4a, full author list expected. The InstructGPT paper has 19 authors.

verdicts.issue: Author names are initials only ('Owens, J.D.', 'Houston, M.', 'Luebke, D.', etc.) per §4a, full first names should be used: John D. Owens, Mike Houston, David Luebke, Simon Green, John E. Stone, James C. Phillips.

verdicts.issue: Key year says 1981 but entry date=1980 and the AFIPS '80 NCC was held May 19-22, 1980. Crossref correctly shows 1980. Mismatch is 1 year — borderline. Also key uses underscores; non-standard convention. Recommend rename to palmer1980_8087.

Title field is just 'BLEU' with the actual paper title in `subtitle` ('a method for automatic evaluation of machine translation'). This produces a misleadingly short rendered title. Also Crossref date=2001 vs key year 2002 — ACL '02 was held July 2002, paper is 2002. pages='311' is the start page only (paper is pp 311-318).

suggested_fix.title: {BLEU}: A Method for Automatic Evaluation of Machine Translation

verdicts.issue: Schema confusion: entry type @article with journal='Journal of Parallel and Distributed Computing' is correct, but a stray booktitle field '2009 IEEE International Symposium on Parallel Distributed Processing' is added — this is from a different paper. Should drop the booktitle field.

suggested_fix.note: Remove stray booktitle field; entry is journal article, not conference proceedings.
